tagline: An interactive 3D globe for exploring geographic data — spin it, zoom it, watch it move.
why: Flat maps quietly lie about the world — distances, sizes, and routes all warp the moment you project a sphere onto a rectangle. I wanted a playground where geographic data sits on an actual globe you can grab and turn, so the relationships between places read the way they really are.
how: The globe is rendered with Three.js for the 3D scene and D3 for the geographic projections and data binding, wired together inside a Next.js app. Interaction and animation are tuned to stay smooth at 60fps even while panning across dense datasets.

features.title: True 3D globe
features.description: Geographic data mapped onto a real sphere with Three.js — no flat-projection distortion.

features.title: Smooth interactivity
features.description: Grab, spin, and zoom with fluid 60fps animation, even across dense datasets.

features.title: Data-driven
features.description: D3 handles the projections and binding, so new geographic layers drop straight in.
